title: Najważniejsze czasowniki
content: {#Wprowadzenie#}{Czasowniki są *rdzeniem zdania* – bez nich nie powiesz, co ktoś robi, co się dzieje lub jak się czuję. Na poziomie A2/A2+ (poziom E8) warto znać *grupę najczęściej używanych czasowników*, które umożliwiają *proste komunikowanie się w życiu codziennym*. Nie podaję tu wszystkich czasowników, tylko te absolutnie kluczowe.}{#''TO BE'' – być#}{[=+Funkcja: opisywanie stanu, miejsca, tożsamości=+/=+Formy: am, is, are=+/]}{Formalna definicja (wg. Cambridge Dictionary):}{[=+used to say something about a person, thing, or state, to show a permanent or temporary quality, state, job, etc.=+/=++He is rich.=+/=++It's cold today.=+/=+used to show the position of a person or thing in space or time=+/=++The food was already on the table.=+/=++Is anyone there?=+/=+used to show what something is made of=+/=++Is this plate pure gold?=+/=+used to say that someone should or must do something=+/=++You're to sit in the corner and keep quiet.=+/=++There's no money left - whare are we to do?=+/=+used to show that something will happen in the future=+/=++We are to visit Australia in the spring.=+/=++She was never to see (=she never saw) her brother again.=+/=+used in conditional sentences to say what might happen=+/=++If I were to refuse, they'd be very annoyed.=+/=+used to say what can happen=+/=++The exhibition of modern prints is currently to be seen at the City Gallery.=+/=+to exist or live=+/=++Such terrible suffering should never be.=+/]}{#''TO HAVE'' – mieć#}{[=+Funkcja: posiadanie czegoś, doświadczenia.=+/=+Formy: have, has (dla he/she/it)=+/]}{Definicja wg. Cambridge Dictionary:}{[=+to own=+/=++They have a beautiful home.=+/]}{#''TO DO'' – robić#}{[=+Funkcja: wykonywanie czynności; w pytaniach i przeczeniach pomaga tworzyć zdania w Present Simple=+/=+Formy: do, does=+/]}{Definicja wg. Cambridge Dictionary:}{[=+used with another verb to form questions and negative sentences, including negative orders, and sometimes in affirmative sentences for reasons of style=+/=++Where do you work?=+/=++Why don't we have lunch together on Friday?=+/=+used instead of the main verb in questions that are added to the end of a sentence to check information=+/=++You met him at our party, didn't you?=+/=+to make, produce, or create something=+/=++I can't go out tonight - I've got to do my history paper.=+/]}{#''TO GO'' – iść, jechać#}{[=+Funkcja: wyrażanie ruchu, przemieszczanie się=+/=+Formy: go, goes=+/]}{Definicja wg. Cambridge Dictionary:}{[=+to travel or move to another place=+/=++We went into the house.=+/=++We don't go to the cinema very often these days.=+/=+to be in the process of moving=+/=++Can't we go any faster?=+/=++We were going along at about 50 miles an hour.=+/=+to move or travel somewhere in order to do something=+/=++She went to meet Blake at the station.=+/]}{#''TO MAKE'' – robić, tworzyć#}{[=+Funkcja: tworzenie czegoś, przygotowywanie=+/=+Formy: make, makes=+/]}{Definicja wg. Cambridge Dictionary:}{[=+to produce something, often using a particular substance or material=+/=++Do you want me to make some coffee?=+/=+to cause something=+/=++The kids made a real mess in the kitchen.=+/=+to cause to be, to become, or to appear as=+/=++It's the good weather that makes Spain such a popular tourist destination=+/]}{#''TO SAY'' – mówić#}{Say oznacza mówić (ogólnie). Wg. Cambridge Dictionary:}{[=+to pronounce words or sounds, to express a thought, opinion, or suggestion, or to state a fact or instruction=+/=++Small children find it difficult to say long words.=+/]}{#''TELL'' – powiedzieć#}{Tell oznacza powiedzieć komuś coś, zazwyczaj z dopełnieniem. Wg. Cambridge Dictionary:}{[=+to say something to someone, often giving them information or instruction=+/=++Tell me about your holiday then.=+/=+if something tells you something, it gives you information=+/=++What does the survey tell us about the lives of teenagers?=+/]}{#''TO GET'' – dostać się, stać się#}{[=+Funkcja: otrzymywanie, zmiana stanu=+/=+Formy: get, gets=+/]}{Definicja wg. Cambridge Dictionary:}{[=+to obtain, buy, or earn something=+/=++He went to the shop to get some milk.=+/=+to receive or be given something=+/=++I got quite a surprise when I saw her with short hair.=+/=+to go somewhere and bring back someone or something=+/=++Let me go get my glasses.=+/=+to become ill with a disease, virus, etc.=+/=++I got food poisoning at that cheap little seafood restaurant.=+/=+to become or start to be=+/=++He gets really upset if you mention his baldness.=+/]}{#''TO SEE'' – widzieć#}{[=+Znaczenie: dostrzegać, zauważać=+/=+Formy: see, sees=+/]}{Definicja wg. Cambridge Dictionary}{[=+to be conscious of what is around you by using your eyes=+/=++Turn the light on so I can see.=+/=+to watch a film, television programme, etc.=+/=++Did you see that documentary on Channel 4 last night?=+/=++to understand, know, or realize=+/=++I see the club is organizing a theatre trip next month.=+/=++to meet or visit someone, or to visit a place=+/=++We're seeing friends this weekend.=+/]}{#''TO LOOK'' – patrzeć#}{[=+Znaczenie: patrzeć, kierować wzrok=+/=+Formy: look, looks=+/]}{Definicja wg. Cambridge Dictionary:}{[=+to direct your eyes in order to see=+/=++Look! There's grandma.=+/=+to try to find something or someone=+/=++I'm looking for my keyes.=+/=+to appear or seem=+/=++You look well!=+/]}{#''TO WATCH'' – oglądać#}{[=+Znaczenie: oglądać (coś w ruchu)=+/=+Formy: watch, watches=+/]}{Definicja wg. Cambridge Dictionary:}{[=+to look at something for a period of time, especially something that is changing or moving=+/=++I had dinner and watched TV for a couple of hours.=+/]}{#''TO LIKE'' – lubić#}{[=+Funkcja: wyrażanie emocji=+/=+Formy: like, likes=+/]}{Definicja wg. Cambridge Dictionary:}{[=+to enjoy or approve of something or someone=+/=++I like your new haircut.=+/=+used to say politely that you want something=+/=++I think I'd like the soup for my starter.=+/=+similar to; in the same way or manner as=+/=++He looks like his brother.=+/]}{#''TO LOVE'' – kochać#}{[=+Funkcja: wyrażanie emocji=+/=+Formy: love, loves=+/]}{Definicja wg. Cambridge Dictionary:}{[=+to like someone very much and be romantically and sexually attracted to them, or to have strong feelings of liking a friend or person in your family=+/=++I love you.=+/=++I've only ever loved one man.=+/=+to like something very much=+/=++She loves animals.=+/=+used, often in requests, to say that you would very much like something=+/=++I'd love a cup of coffee if you're making one.=+/]}{#''TO HATE'' – nienawidzić#}{[=+Funkcja: wyrażanie emocji=+/=+Formy: hate, hates=+/]}{Definicja wg. Cambridge Dictionary:}{[=+to dislike someone or something very much=+/=++Kelly hates her teacher.=+/=+an extremely strong dislike=+/=++She gave him a look of pure hate.=+/]}{#''TO WANT'' – chcieć#}{[=+to wish for a particular thing or plan of action. ''Want'' is not used in polite requests=+/=++I want some chocolate=+/=+to wish or need someone to be present=+/=++Am I wanted at the meeting tomorrow?=+/=+to need something=+/=++Do you think this soup wants a bit of salt?=+/]}{#''TO NEED'' – potrzebować#}{[=+to have to have something, or to want something very much=+/=++Babies need constant care=+/=+if you say that someone or something needs something else, you mean that they should have it, or would get an advantage from having it=+/=++What you need is a nice hot bowl of soup=+/=+to have to=+/=++I need to do some shopping on my way home from work.=+/]}{#''TO KNOW'' – wiedzieć#}{[=+to have information in your mind=+/=++She knows the name of every kid in the school.=+/=+used to ask someone to tell you a piece of information=+/=++Do you know the time?=+/=+to be certain=+/=++I know she'll be really pleased to hear the news=+/]}{#''TO THINK'' – myśleć#}{[=+to believe something or have an opinion or idea=+/=++I think I've met you before.=+/=+to consider a person's needs or wishes=+/=++She's always thinking of others=+/=+to use the brain to decide to do something=+/=++I'm thinking of taking up running=+/]}
